message:
feat(abi): port 14 NIS / yp_* fail-safe libnsl stubs (bd-v9bcs)

NIS / Yellow Pages is dead infrastructure; modern Linux systems do
not run ypbind. These wrappers exist so binaries that statically
reference yp_* through libc do not fail at link time, and a graceful
YPERR_NODOM (= 12, "local NIS domain name not set") or YPERR_DOMAIN
(= 3, "can't bind to server which serves this domain") is returned at
runtime. Programs that link libnsl.so.1 explicitly still get the real
impls there.

  - yp_get_default_domain(**outdomain) → write NULL, return YPERR_NODOM.
  - yp_bind(*dom) → YPERR_DOMAIN.
  - yp_unbind(*dom) → no-op.
  - yp_match / yp_first / yp_next / yp_all / yp_master / yp_order /
    yp_maplist / yp_update → zero outputs, return YPERR_DOMAIN.
  - yperr_string(err) and ypbinderr_string(err) → static description
    strings (Success / "local domain name not set" / etc.).
  - ypprot_err(code) → 0 for code == 0; YPERR_YPBIND otherwise.

Tests: 14 abi tests covering YPERR_* return codes, output-pointer
zeroing across all the lookup variants, the no-callback-invocation
guarantee in yp_all, and exact text matches for known yperr_string /
ypbinderr_string codes plus an "unknown" fallback for out-of-range
values.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
